edges.description: AP1S1 dysfunction impairs intracellular trafficking of copper transport machinery.
AP1S1 dysfunction impairs epithelial junctional integrity and barrier function.
AP1S1 loss causes the syndromic neurodevelopmental phenotype that includes intellectual disability.
AP1S1 loss causes the syndromic neurocutaneous phenotype that includes deafness.
AP1S1 loss causes the syndromic neurocutaneous phenotype that includes peripheral neuropathy.
AP1S1 loss causes the syndromic neurocutaneous phenotype that includes ichthyosis.
AP1S1 loss causes the syndromic neurocutaneous phenotype that includes palmoplantar keratoderma.
Additional AP1S1 pathogenic variants expand the neurological phenotype to include seizures.
AP1S1-related MEDNIK syndrome can include yellow or pale hair pigmentation.
AP1S1-related MEDNIK syndrome can include a high or prominent forehead.
Defective copper pump trafficking produces the Wilson-like hepatic copper-overload branch of MEDNIK syndrome.
intermediate_mechanisms: Mislocalization of ATP7A and ATP7B copper pumps

edges.description: Hepatic copper overload is measured as increased liver copper.
Barrier dysfunction explains the enteropathy and chronic/intractable diarrhea phenotype in MEDNIK syndrome.
intermediate_mechanisms: Tight-junction protein mislocalization and increased epithelial permeability

edges.description: Increased liver copper reports the Wilson-like hepatic copper-overload branch downstream of AP1S1-dependent copper-pump trafficking defects.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and Genetic Functional Validation of a Novel AP1S1 Mutation Causing MEDNIK Syndrome
evidence.snippet: This mutation causes a frameshift mutation in the protein, altering the protein structure and affecting protein function.

nodes.description: MEDNIK syndrome is caused by pathogenic AP1S1 variants that disrupt the adaptor protein complex 1 and intracellular trafficking.

evidence.reference_title: AP1S1 defect causing MEDNIK syndrome: a new adaptinopathy associated with defective copper metabolism
evidence.snippet: MEDNIK syndrome is caused by mutation of the AP1S1 gene

nodes.description: Enteropathy with chronic or intractable diarrhea is a major gastrointestinal manifestation of MEDNIK syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and Genetic Functional Validation of a Novel AP1S1 Mutation Causing MEDNIK Syndrome
evidence.snippet: Furthermore, the sister initially presented with intractable diarrhoea

nodes.description: AP1S1 directs intracellular trafficking of ATP7A and ATP7B, linking MEDNIK syndrome to disordered copper handling.

evidence.reference_title: AP1S1 defect causing MEDNIK syndrome: a new adaptinopathy associated with defective copper metabolism
evidence.snippet: MEDNIK syndrome is caused by mutation of the AP1S1 gene, which codes for the σ1A subunit of adaptor protein complex 1, and directs intracellular trafficking of copper pumps ATP7A and ATP7B.

nodes.description: Abnormally pale or yellow hair has been reported in affected siblings with MEDNIK syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and Genetic Functional Validation of a Novel AP1S1 Mutation Causing MEDNIK Syndrome
evidence.snippet: The proband and his sister exhibited developmental delays, seizures, yellow hair, sparse teeth and a high forehead.

nodes.description: Hearing loss is part of the defining syndrome phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Disruption of AP1S1, causing a novel neurocutaneous syndrome, perturbs development of the skin and spinal cord.
evidence.snippet: enteropathy, deafness, peripheral neuropathy, ichthyosis, and keratodermia

nodes.description: MEDNIK syndrome includes Wilson-like hepatic copper overload downstream of defective AP1S1-dependent copper pump trafficking.

evidence.reference_title: AP1S1 defect causing MEDNIK syndrome: a new adaptinopathy associated with defective copper metabolism
evidence.snippet: This multisystem disease combines clinical and biochemical signs of both Menkes and Wilson's diseases, in which liver copper overload is treatable using zinc acetate therapy.
